message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state from concurrent agent work

Updates the beads JSONL issue database to reflect status changes made
by other agents working on the project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2tnl.4.5","title":"T4.5: Implement background embedding/index refresh jobs with retries","description":"## Task
Implement background semantic indexing jobs and incremental refresh.

## Scope
- batch scheduling + backpressure.
- incremental updates on write events.
- rebuild hooks using Track 2 interfaces.
- retry/failure bookkeeping.

## Deliverable
Operational semantic indexing pipeline with bounded latency impact.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Background refresh jobs update embeddings and indexes with retry and backoff behavior.
2. Job orchestration avoids duplicate work and maintains freshness guarantees under load.
3. Tests validate retry handling, convergence, and observability outputs.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.859530068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:43:16.566287279Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:43:16.566268434Z","close_reason":"Completed: retry-convergence and semantic-indexing observability coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["background-jobs","search","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:45.882130997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:03.859530068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:41.488373829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5","title":"[track] Hybrid Fusion, Two-Tier Refinement, and Reranking","description":"## Purpose
Implement hybrid search quality stack: lexical + semantic candidate fusion, progressive two-tier refinement, and reranking.

## Scope
- Candidate generation orchestration.
- RRF fusion with deterministic tie-breakers.
- Two-tier fast→quality refinement path.
- Cross-encoder reranking and score blending.
- Explainable score decomposition and cache/warmup behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Candidate orchestration + RRF fusion + two-tier refinement are implemented.
- Optional reranker integration works with fallback behavior.
- Hybrid responses include merged explainability metadata and cache/warmup controls.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"track","assignee":"CloudyTiger","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:26.068774755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hybrid","quality","reranking","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.533469337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:35.240065196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.432717942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:36.633790517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":233,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 5 reasoning:

- Hybrid quality gains come from principled fusion (RRF) and progressive refinement, not from ad-hoc score mixing.
- Two-tier search is specifically chosen to protect latency while still allowing high-quality refinement.
- Reranker is optional-at-runtime but mandatory-in-design for top-end precision.
- Explainability is required so operators can trust why a result ranked highly, especially when multiple scoring stages are combined.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:39:13Z"},{"id":690,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5","author":"AzureBarn","text":"Coordination update: triaged with bv --robot-triage/--robot-plan and claimed this track (status=in_progress). Active child owners detected: br-2tnl.5.1 (IvoryMeadow), br-2tnl.5.10.2 (AzureHarbor). I am selecting an unblocked child next to avoid overlap and will post artifacts + diagnostics in-thread.","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.1","title":"T5.1: Build hybrid candidate orchestration (lexical + semantic pools)","description":"## Task
Implement candidate retrieval orchestrator for hybrid mode.

## Scope
- lexical candidate retrieval sizing.
- semantic candidate retrieval sizing.
- mode-aware candidate multipliers.
- deterministic dedupe and merge preparation.

## Deliverable
Hybrid candidate selection stage used by fusion/rerank pipeline.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Candidate orchestration merges lexical and semantic pools with clear stage boundaries.
2. Candidate budgets and quotas are configurable per mode and query class.
3. Tests validate orchestration correctness and deterministic ordering of candidate sets.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Progress: converted candidate budget arithmetic in hybrid_candidates to deterministic integer-scaled math (no float->usize casts), kept mode-aware/query-class-aware derivation, and revalidated module tests + db search-v3 check. Coordination updates posted in Agent Mail thread br-2tnl.5.1.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.125917254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:16:38.067327253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["candidates","hybrid","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:46.090797759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:46.304537776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:04.125917254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2tnl.5.10","title":"Two-Tier Search Hardening: Thread-Safe Init, Embedder Fallbacks, Progressive Refinement","description":"## Background

Code review of the two-tier search implementation identified three areas for hardening:

1. **Lazy initialization race condition** - `try_two_tier_search` may create duplicate `TwoTierBridge` instances when called concurrently before initialization completes.

2. **Zero-filled quality embeddings** - When quality embedder is unavailable, documents get zero-filled quality embeddings which provide no meaningful similarity signal.

3. **Progressive refinement not implemented** - The current `TwoTierBridge::search()` only uses fast embeddings; the full fast→quality progressive refinement workflow is stubbed.

## Goals

- Ensure thread-safe single initialization of embedder bridges
- Provide meaningful fallback behavior when quality embedder is unavailable
- Implement full progressive search with streaming/async quality refinement
- Maintain sub-millisecond fast-path latency
- Add comprehensive observability for debugging and monitoring

## Architecture Context

The two-tier system uses:
- **Fast tier**: potion-128M (Model2Vec, ~0ms inference, 256 dims)
- **Quality tier**: MiniLM-L6-v2 (FastEmbed/ONNX, ~128ms, 384 dims)

Both embedders use global `OnceLock` singletons for auto-initialization.

## Files of Interest

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs` - Core index and search
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/auto_init.rs` - Global context
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/model2vec.rs` - Fast embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/fastembed.rs` - Quality embedder
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs` - TwoTierBridge integration

## Success Criteria

- Zero duplicate bridge creation under concurrent access
- Quality-unavailable documents still participate in semantic search meaningfully
- Progressive refinement yields improved relevance with transparent latency
- All tests pass with -D warnings clippy","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:04.614473056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","search","semantic","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:27.353873587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":514,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started work on child br-2tnl.5.10.2 (forced in_progress due blocked parent-child constraints). Implementing quality-embedding fallback hardening and keeping validations attached to that child.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:07:14Z"},{"id":523,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OVERALL SUCCESS CRITERIA CHECKLIST (aggregate of child beads):

## Core Implementation (P0)
- [ ] T5.10.1: Race condition fixed - only one bridge created under concurrent access
- [ ] T5.10.2: Quality fallback - has_quality flag implemented, no zero-vectors stored
- [ ] T5.10.3: Progressive refinement - search yields Initial then Refined phases

## Testing (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.4: 7+ unit tests for thread-safe init (including 100-thread stress test)
- [ ] T5.10.5: 8+ unit tests for quality fallback (including boundary tests)
- [ ] T5.10.6: 12+ unit tests for progressive refinement (including timeout, edge cases)
- [ ] T5.10.7: E2E script passes all 7 test functions
- [ ] T5.10.10: Benchmarks pass performance targets (<5ms fast search for 10K docs)

## Observability (P1)
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 4 metrics structs implemented
- [ ] T5.10.8: All 8 tracing spans emitted
- [ ] T5.10.8: User-friendly messaging for degraded mode

## Optimization (P2)
- [ ] T5.10.9: Baseline established before optimization
- [ ] T5.10.9: At least one optimization phase completed with measurable improvement

## User Experience
- [ ] Search results include was_refined and explain fields
- [ ] Health check reports two-tier status with install hints if degraded
- [ ] TUI/CLI shows quality coverage stats
- [ ] Configuration via env vars (TWO_TIER_FAST_ONLY, TWO_TIER_QUALITY_TIMEOUT_MS, etc.)

## Definition of Done
All P0 and P1 items complete. P2 items can be deferred to follow-up.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:30:05Z"},{"id":524,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL GAPS IDENTIFIED FROM CODE REVIEW:

1. **INDEX PERSISTENCE**: Current implementation is in-memory only. On restart, all embeddings are lost and must be recomputed. Consider:
   - Persist index to disk (memmap or sqlite blob)
   - Or accept recompute-on-restart for MVP

2. **INDEX SIZE LIMITS**: No bounds on index growth. For large projects:
   - Add configurable MAX_INDEX_DOCS (default: 100K)
   - Implement LRU eviction for oldest/least-accessed docs
   - Add metric for index memory usage

3. **DOCUMENT UPDATES/REMOVAL**: No API to update or remove docs from index:
   - add_document() always appends
   - Need remove_document(doc_id) for deleted messages
   - Need update_document() or remove+add

4. **LARGE DOCUMENT HANDLING**: Embedding very long texts:
   - Model2Vec has implicit token limit (~512 tokens)
   - FastEmbed truncates at 256 tokens by default
   - Add MAX_EMBED_TEXT_LENGTH config (default: 4096 chars)
   - Truncate or chunk long documents

5. **EMBEDDER HEALTH DURING SESSION**: Code at line 806 checks is_available() but:
   - What if embedder crashes mid-session?
   - Add periodic health check and recovery
   - Log warning if embedder becomes unavailable

6. **LEGACY FALLBACK TESTING**: Line 815 falls back to SemanticBridge:
   - Ensure T5.10.7 E2E tests this fallback path
   - Test: disable two-tier, verify legacy semantic still works","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:32:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","title":"T5.10.1: Eliminate lazy initialization race in TwoTierBridge","description":"## Problem Statement

The current `try_two_tier_search` function (search_service.rs:776-793) has a race condition:

```rust
fn try_two_tier_search(query: &SearchQuery, limit: usize) -> Option<Vec<SearchResult>> {
    let bridge = get_two_tier_bridge().or_else(|| {
        let _ = init_two_tier_bridge();  // ← Race: multiple threads may call this
        get_two_tier_bridge()
    });
    // ...
}
```

When two threads call `try_two_tier_search` before initialization:
1. Both threads see `get_two_tier_bridge()` return `None`
2. Both threads call `init_two_tier_bridge()`
3. Both create a `TwoTierBridge` (expensive: loads embedders, allocates index)
4. `OnceLock::set` silently drops the second bridge
5. Duplicate "Two-tier semantic bridge initialized" log messages appear

## Impact

- **Resource waste**: Creating TwoTierBridge is expensive (embedder initialization)
- **Confusing logs**: Duplicate initialization messages
- **Theoretical correctness**: Both threads still get a valid bridge, but wasteful

## Solution: OnceLock::get_or_init Pattern

Replace the two-step "check then init" pattern with atomic `get_or_init`:

```rust
static TWO_TIER_BRIDGE: OnceLock<Arc<TwoTierBridge>> = OnceLock::new();

fn get_or_init_two_tier_bridge() -> &'static Arc<TwoTierBridge> {
    TWO_TIER_BRIDGE.get_or_init(|| {
        Arc::new(TwoTierBridge::new())
    })
}
```

However, `TwoTierBridge::new()` may fail if embedders are unavailable. Need to handle:
- `OnceLock<Option<Arc<TwoTierBridge>>>` for fallible init
- Or separate `OnceLock` for availability check

## Implementation Plan

1. Change `TWO_TIER_BRIDGE` to use `get_or_init` pattern
2. Handle fallible initialization gracefully
3. Remove `init_two_tier_bridge()` public API (no longer needed)
4. Update `try_two_tier_search` to use atomic getter
5. Add debug logging for initialization timing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Only one TwoTierBridge created under concurrent access
- [ ] Single "Two-tier semantic bridge initialized" log message
- [ ] No change to search behavior or latency
- [ ] Existing tests pass

## Test Plan

1. Unit test: concurrent access to `get_or_init_two_tier_bridge` from 10 threads
2. Verify only one initialization log message
3. Verify all threads get same Arc pointer (pointer equality)

## Files to Modify

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`
  - `TWO_TIER_BRIDGE` static
  - `init_two_tier_bridge()` → deprecate/remove
  - `get_two_tier_bridge()` → use `get_or_init`
  - `try_two_tier_search()` → simplify","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:46.402926417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:51:39.726160272Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T01:51:39.726140074Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified in search_service.rs (atomic OnceLock get_or_init + concurrency tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","init","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:38:46.402926417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":490,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: The OnceLock pattern change should be backwards-compatible. Existing code calling init_two_tier_bridge() can be deprecated with #[deprecated] attribute, pointing to get_or_init_two_tier_bridge(). The test for this should verify Arc pointer equality across threads, not just functional correctness.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:43:22Z"},{"id":508,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (CopperTower): Fixed race condition using OnceLock::get_or_init pattern. Added get_or_init_two_tier_bridge() for atomic TwoTierBridge init. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:54:40Z"},{"id":516,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes audit follow-up: get_or_init_two_tier_bridge_is_thread_safe test now asserts full completion of all 10 spawned threads before checking Option/pointer invariants, preventing partial-result false positives.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:14:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.10","title":"T5.10.10: Benchmark suite for two-tier search performance","description":"## Objective

Comprehensive benchmark suite to validate two-tier search performance targets and prevent regressions.

## Background: Performance Requirements

From the bake-off and production requirements:
- **Fast embedding**: <1ms for any query
- **Fast search (1K docs)**: <1ms
- **Fast search (10K docs)**: <5ms
- **Fast search (100K docs)**: <50ms
- **Quality refinement**: <200ms additional
- **Memory**: <1KB per document (fast + quality embeddings)

## Benchmark Categories

### 1. Micro-benchmarks (per-operation)

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_dot_product_f16_256dim(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedding: Vec<f16> = (0..256).map(|i| f16::from_f32(i as f32 * 0.01)).collect();
    let query: Vec<f32> = (0..256).map(|i| i as f32 * 0.01).collect();
    
    b.iter(|| {
        black_box(dot_product_f16_simd(black_box(&embedding), black_box(&query)))
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_l2_normalize_256dim(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let mut vec: Vec<f32> = (0..256).map(|i| i as f32 * 0.01).collect();
    b.iter(|| {
        l2_normalize(black_box(&mut vec.clone()));
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_f16_to_f32_conversion_256(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedding: Vec<f16> = (0..256).map(|i| f16::from_f32(i as f32 * 0.01)).collect();
    b.iter(|| {
        let _: Vec<f32> = black_box(&embedding).iter().map(|&x| f32::from(x)).collect();
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Index benchmarks (search operations)

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_100_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(100);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_1000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(1000);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_fast_10000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(10000);
    let query = make_random_query(256);
    b.iter(|| black_box(index.search_fast(black_box(&query), 10)));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_search_progressive_1000_docs(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let index = build_test_index(1000);
    let searcher = create_test_searcher(&index);
    let query = "test query for progressive search";
    
    b.iter(|| {
        let phases: Vec<_> = searcher.search(black_box(query), 10).collect();
        black_box(phases)
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Embedding benchmarks

\`\`\`rust
#[bench]
fn bench_fast_embedding_short_text(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_fast_embedder().expect("fast embedder required");
    let text = "short query";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}

#[bench]
fn bench_fast_embedding_long_text(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_fast_embedder().expect("fast embedder required");
    let text = "This is a much longer piece of text that simulates a typical message body with multiple sentences and various technical terms that might appear in agent mail communications.";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}

#[bench]
#[ignore = "requires quality embedder"]
fn bench_quality_embedding(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let embedder = get_quality_embedder().expect("quality embedder required");
    let text = "test query for quality embedding";
    b.iter(|| black_box(embedder.embed(black_box(text)).unwrap()));
}
\`\`\`

### 4. Memory benchmarks

\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_memory_per_document() {
    let config = TwoTierConfig::default();
    let mut index = TwoTierIndex::new(&config);
    
    // Baseline memory
    let baseline = get_memory_usage();
    
    // Add 1000 documents
    for i in 0..1000 {
        index.add_entry(make_test_entry(i)).unwrap();
    }
    
    let after = get_memory_usage();
    let per_doc = (after - baseline) / 1000;
    
    println!("Memory per document: {} bytes", per_doc);
    assert!(per_doc < 1024, "should be <1KB per doc, was {} bytes", per_doc);
}
\`\`\`

## Benchmark Script: scripts/bench_two_tier.sh

\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Two-Tier Search Performance Benchmark Suite
# Runs all benchmarks and generates report

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
RESULTS_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/benchmark_results/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"

mkdir -p "$RESULTS_DIR"

echo "=== Two-Tier Search Benchmark Suite ==="
echo "Results directory: $RESULTS_DIR"
echo ""

# Run Criterion benchmarks
echo "Running Criterion benchmarks..."
cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core \
    --bench two_tier_bench \
    -- --save-baseline current 2>&1 | tee "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log"

# Extract key metrics
echo ""
echo "=== Key Performance Metrics ==="

# Parse Criterion output for key benchmarks
grep -E "bench_search_fast|bench_dot_product|bench_.*_embedding" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | \
    grep -E "time:.*\[" || true

# Memory analysis
echo ""
echo "Running memory analysis..."
cargo test --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core \
    test_memory_per_document \
    -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee "$RESULTS_DIR/memory.log"

# Generate summary JSON
cat > "$RESULTS_DIR/summary.json" << EOF
{
  "timestamp": "$(date -Iseconds)",
  "git_commit": "$(git rev-parse HEAD)",
  "benchmarks": {
    "dot_product_256dim_ns": $(grep "bench_dot_product_f16_256dim" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "search_fast_1k_us": $(grep "bench_search_fast_1000" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "search_fast_10k_us": $(grep "bench_search_fast_10000" "$RESULTS_DIR/criterion.log" | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+' | head -1 || echo "null"),
    "memory_per_doc_bytes": $(grep "Memory per document" "$RESULTS_DIR/memory.log" | grep -oP '\d+' | head -1 || echo "null")
  },
  "targets": {
    "search_fast_1k_us": 1000,
    "search_fast_10k_us": 5000,
    "memory_per_doc_bytes": 1024
  }
}
EOF

echo ""
echo "Summary written to: $RESULTS_DIR/summary.json"
cat "$RESULTS_DIR/summary.json"
\`\`\`

## CI Integration

Add to .github/workflows/bench.yml:

\`\`\`yaml
name: Benchmarks
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/**'

jobs:
  benchmark:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
      - name: Run benchmarks
        run: cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core -- --save-baseline pr
      - name: Compare with main
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: |
          cargo bench --package mcp-agent-mail-search-core -- --baseline main --save-baseline pr
\`\`\`

## Logging and Diagnostics

Each benchmark run should emit:

\`\`\`
[BENCH] two_tier.dot_product_256: 45ns ± 2ns (1000 iterations)
[BENCH] two_tier.search_fast_1k: 892µs ± 34µs (100 iterations)
[BENCH] two_tier.search_progressive_1k: 145ms ± 12ms (10 iterations)
  └─ [PHASE] initial: 1.2ms
  └─ [PHASE] refinement: 143.8ms
[BENCH] two_tier.memory_per_doc: 756 bytes
[CHECK] ✓ All performance targets met
\`\`\`

## Performance Regression Detection

Fail CI if:
- Fast search >2x slower than baseline
- Memory per doc >1.5x larger than target
- Progressive refinement >300ms

## Files to Create

- \`crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/benches/two_tier_bench.rs\` - Criterion benchmarks
- \`scripts/bench_two_tier.sh\` - Benchmark runner script
- \`.github/workflows/bench.yml\` - CI integration (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All micro-benchmarks implemented with Criterion
- [ ] Index benchmarks cover 100, 1K, 10K doc sizes
- [ ] Memory benchmark validates <1KB/doc target
- [ ] Benchmark script produces structured JSON output
- [ ] Detailed logging shows per-phase timings
- [ ] CI integration detects regressions (optional)","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:55:12.426986332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:14.207135721Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","testing","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.10","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:55:12.426986332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.10","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:55:19.053828998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":519,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BENCHMARK REPRODUCIBILITY REQUIREMENTS:

1. WARM-UP: All benchmarks must include warm-up iterations to ensure:
   - CPU frequency scaling has stabilized
   - Caches are populated
   - JIT/branch prediction is trained
   - Criterion handles this automatically, but verify with --warm-up-time 3

2. ENVIRONMENT CONTROL:
   - Document CPU model, memory, OS version in results
   - Pin process to specific CPU cores: taskset -c 0,1 cargo bench
   - Disable CPU frequency scaling during benchmarks: cpupower frequency-set -g performance
   - Close background applications

3. NOISE HANDLING:
   - Run each benchmark multiple times (Criterion default: 100 samples)
   - Report median and IQR, not just mean
   - Use Criterion's change detection: cargo bench -- --baseline main
   - Flag any result with >10% variance for manual review

4. DETERMINISTIC SEED:
   - Use fixed random seed for test data generation:
```rust
fn build_test_index(size: usize) -> TwoTierIndex {
    let mut rng = StdRng::seed_from_u64(42);  // Fixed seed for reproducibility
    // ...
}
```

5. REGRESSION DETECTION THRESHOLDS:
   - P50 latency regression >20%: WARNING
   - P95 latency regression >30%: WARNING
   - Any metric regression >50%: FAIL CI
   - Memory per doc increase >25%: FAIL CI","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:29:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","title":"T5.10.2: Implement meaningful fallback for missing quality embeddings","description":"## Problem Statement

When the quality embedder is unavailable (e.g., MiniLM not downloaded), documents get zero-filled quality embeddings (search_service.rs:717-721):

```rust
let quality_embedding_f16 = quality_embedding
    .unwrap_or_else(|| vec![0.0; self.config.quality_dimension])  // ← All zeros!
    .into_iter()
    .map(f16::from_f32)
    .collect::<Vec<_>>();
```

**Problem**: A zero vector has no meaningful similarity with any query:
- `dot_product([0,0,...,0], query) = 0` regardless of query
- During quality refinement, all zero-embedded docs get score 0
- Score blending becomes meaningless: `0.3 * fast + 0.7 * 0 = 0.3 * fast`

## Impact

- **Degraded refinement**: Quality tier contributes nothing for zero-embedded docs
- **Ranking distortion**: Fast-only scores dominate even when quality should matter
- **Silent failure**: No indication that quality embeddings are missing

## Solution Options

### Option A: Marker-Based Exclusion (Recommended)

Store a "has_quality_embedding" flag per document:

```rust
pub struct TwoTierEntry {
    pub doc_id: u64,
    pub doc_kind: DocKind,
    pub project_id: Option<i64>,
    pub fast_embedding: Vec<f16>,
    pub quality_embedding: Vec<f16>,
    pub has_quality: bool,  // ← New field
}
```

During refinement, skip quality scoring for docs without quality embeddings:
- Fast score only for these docs
- Or exclude from quality-dependent ranking entirely

### Option B: Replicate Fast Embedding as Quality Fallback

When quality is unavailable, project fast embedding into quality space:
- Simple: pad/truncate fast to quality dimension (lossy but non-zero)
- Better: learn a projection matrix (requires training data)

### Option C: Hash-Based Pseudo-Embedding

Use deterministic hash of text to generate a pseudo-embedding:
- `hashembed(text) → [f32; quality_dim]`
- Provides some clustering by text similarity
- Already implemented in `HashEmbedder`

## Recommended Approach: Option A + Graceful Degradation

1. Add `has_quality: bool` to `TwoTierEntry`
2. Store `true` only when real quality embedding is computed
3. During refinement:
   - Docs with `has_quality=true`: use blended score
   - Docs with `has_quality=false`: use fast score only (weight=0 for quality)
4. Add observability: track ratio of quality-available docs

## Implementation Plan

1. **Modify TwoTierEntry**: Add `has_quality` field
2. **Modify TwoTierIndex**: Store `has_quality` in parallel vector
3. **Modify add_document**: Set `has_quality` based on actual embedding success
4. **Modify refinement logic**: Skip quality scoring for non-quality docs
5. **Add metrics**: Track quality embedding coverage percentage
6. **Add tests**: Verify graceful degradation behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Documents without quality embeddings participate in fast search
- [ ] Quality refinement correctly excludes/handles non-quality docs
- [ ] Metrics show quality embedding coverage ratio
- [ ] No zero-vector quality embeddings stored
- [ ] Existing tests pass

## Test Plan

1. Unit test: Add docs with and without quality embeddings
2. Unit test: Search returns correct results mixing both types
3. Unit test: Refinement doesn't crash on non-quality docs
4. Integration test: Verify quality coverage metric

## Files to Modify

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-search-core/src/two_tier.rs`
  - `TwoTierEntry` struct
  - `TwoTierIndex` struct
  - `add_entry()` method
  - Refinement scoring logic

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`
  - `TwoTierBridge::add_document()` method","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Missing-quality-embedding fallback is implemented with deterministic behavior and explicit degrade mode messaging
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests validate trigger conditions, fallback scoring semantics, and edge-case handling
- [ ] Integration/E2E search scenarios cover fallback activation, recovery when embeddings return, and user-visible relevance continuity
- [ ] Structured diagnostics capture fallback reason codes, quality score impacts, latency deltas, and replay metadata","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:39:08.998795938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:33:44.864027168Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embeddings","fallback","progressive","semantic","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:39:08.998795938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":491,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN DECISION: Option A (marker-based exclusion with has_quality flag) is recommended over Option B (projection) or Option C (hash fallback) because it's explicit, doesn't add latency, and allows clear metrics on quality coverage. The hash fallback is already available in HashEmbedder if needed as a future enhancement.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:43:25Z"},{"id":507,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MIGRATION STRATEGY for existing zero-vector documents:

1. DETECTION: Add utility function to scan index for zero-vector quality embeddings:
```rust
impl TwoTierIndex {
    pub fn detect_zero_quality_docs(&self) -> Vec<u64> {
        self.entries.iter()
            .filter(|e| e.has_quality && is_zero_vector(&e.quality_embedding))
            .map(|e| e.doc_id)
            .collect()
    }
}
```

2. BACKFILL OPTION A (recommended): Mark existing zero-vectors as has_quality=false:
```rust
pub fn migrate_zero_quality_to_no_quality(&mut self) -> usize {
    let mut count = 0;
    for entry in &mut self.entries {
        if entry.has_quality && is_zero_vector(&entry.quality_embedding) {
            entry.has_quality = false;
            count += 1;
        }
    }
    tracing::info!(migrated = count, "Migrated zero-quality docs to has_quality=false");
    count
}
```

3. BACKFILL OPTION B (if quality embedder now available): Re-embed with quality:
- This requires storing original text or re-fetching from DB
- More expensive but produces real quality embeddings
- Implement as optional background job

4. AUTO-MIGRATION ON STARTUP: Call migrate_zero_quality_to_no_quality() on index load if any zero-vectors detected. Log count for observability.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:06Z"},{"id":512,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Picked up via bv triage. Implemented has_quality wiring and zero-vector fallback handling fixes in two_tier/search_service; compile failure from missing field is resolved. Running validation + clippy cleanup in touched crates.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:04:54Z"},{"id":513,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-db -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db two_tier_entry_contract, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --features hybrid two_tier::tests::test_has_quality_flag, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools summarize_mention_. Full workspace clippy still red due large pre-existing server lint backlog. Agent Mail MCP/CLI remains blocked by SQLite lock/timeouts (ensure_project database is locked), so cross-agent mail replies are currently blocked.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:06:43Z"},{"id":517,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fresh-eyes pass: tightened TwoTierBridge concurrency test assertions in search_service.rs to require all spawned threads complete (len==10) before pointer-equality checks; avoids false-positive pass when a join is dropped. Revalidated with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db two_tier_entry_contract, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools summarize_mention_.","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:14:22Z"},{"id":520,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"USER VISIBILITY INTO QUALITY EMBEDDING STATUS:

The has_quality flag is internal, but users may want to know:
- Which documents have quality embeddings
- Overall quality coverage in their project
- Whether a search result was refined or fast-only

1. ADD TO SEARCH RESULTS:
```rust
pub struct SearchResult {
    pub doc_id: u64,
    pub score: f32,
    pub was_refined: bool,  // true if quality scoring was applied
    pub explain: Option<SearchExplain>,  // includes quality details
}

pub struct SearchExplain {
    pub fast_score: f32,
    pub quality_score: Option<f32>,  // None if has_quality=false
    pub blend_weight: f32,
    pub final_score: f32,
}
```

2. ADD TO INDEX STATS (exposed via TUI and API):
```json
{
  "total_docs": 1234,
  "quality_doc_count": 890,
  "quality_coverage_percent": 72.1,
  "fast_embedder": "potion-128M",
  "quality_embedder": "MiniLM-L6-v2"
}
```

3. ADD CLI COMMAND:
```bash
am search-stats --project /path/to/project
# Output:
# Two-Tier Search Index Stats:
#   Total documents: 1,234
#   With quality embeddings: 890 (72.1%)
#   Fast embedder: potion-128M (available)
#   Quality embedder: MiniLM-L6-v2 (available)
```","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:29:16Z"},{"id":535,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.5.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION STATUS NOTE:

Code review shows has_quality field ALREADY EXISTS:
- TwoTierEntry has 'pub has_quality: bool' (two_tier.rs:131)
- TwoTierIndex has 'has_quality_flags: Vec<bool>' (two_tier.rs:218)
- is_zero_vector_f16() utility exists (two_tier.rs:228)

HOWEVER, search_service.rs:719-720 still creates zero vectors:
```rust
let quality_embedding_f16 = quality_embedding
    .unwrap_or_else(|| vec\![0.0; self.config.quality_dimension])  // ← STILL ZEROS\!
```

REMAINING WORK for T5.10.2:
1. Change to NOT store zero vectors - store empty or skip quality_embedding field entirely
2. Ensure refinement scoring checks has_quality_flags before computing quality score
3. Add quality_coverage_ratio() method to TwoTierIndex
4. Add migration for existing zero-vector docs (see earlier migration comment)
5. Update search_service to not call unwrap_or_else with zeros

+{"id":"br-2z8jq","title":"T9.1: Implement Message Compose form with validation and autocomplete","description":"Create a modal form for composing new messages. This is the highest-value form because
message composition is the most common write operation.

FORM FIELDS:
- To: TextInput with autocomplete (registered agents in current project)
- CC: TextInput with autocomplete (optional, comma-separated)
- Subject: TextInput (required, max 200 chars)
- Thread ID: TextInput (optional, auto-generated if blank)
- Body: TextArea (markdown supported, minimum 10 rows)
- Importance: Select dropdown (Normal, High, Urgent)
- Ack Required: Checkbox (default: false)

AUTOCOMPLETE:
- As user types agent name, show dropdown with matching agents
- Use existing build_palette_actions_from_snapshot() agent list
- Arrow keys select from dropdown, Enter confirms

VALIDATION:
- To: must be registered agent name
- Subject: required, 1-200 chars
- Body: required, non-empty
- Show inline validation errors in red below field

SUBMISSION:
- Ctrl+Enter or F5 submits
- Calls send_message MCP tool internally
- Shows success toast on completion
- Shows error toast on failure

KEYBINDING: 'c' (compose) opens form as modal overlay.

FILES: tui_screens/messages.rs or new compose module, tui_app.rs (keybinding)","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] 'c' opens compose form as modal
- [ ] All fields render and accept input
- [ ] Autocomplete works for agent names
- [ ] Validation prevents submission of invalid data
- [ ] Ctrl+Enter submits message
- [ ] Success/error toasts on completion
- [ ] Escape cancels form

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyBadger","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:13:54.867518197Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["forms","messaging","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2z8jq","depends_on_id":"br-1ityn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:13.276380131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2z8jq","depends_on_id":"br-2qycc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:41.942500100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2zd11","title":"T9.2: Git lock, OS, and resource exhaustion error parity","description":"GIT_INDEX_LOCK (recoverable=true): message with attempts count, lock_path in data. RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (recoverable=true): message with freed count. OS_ERROR (recoverable=false): simple message with errno in data. All messages and data payloads must match.","notes":"GIT_INDEX_LOCK: Rust uses short message (no attempts count - architectural difference, Rust doesn't retry at tool layer). RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED: Message includes resource type. OS_ERROR: Maps from io::Error. All error types and recoverable flags match Python.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:04:37.606613499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:30:06.495075174Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:30:06.494972382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
 {"id":"br-2zq8o","title":"D.3: Frame-time benchmarks + conformal coverage validation","description":"**Background**

The Bayesian diff strategy must be validated against frame-time targets and the conformal prediction intervals (from Track G, if available) must be verified.

**Scope / Adoption wedge**

1. Create `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/benches/frame_bench.rs` using criterion:
   - `bench_frame_full_diff` -- render 100 frames with full diff, measure p50/p95/p99
   - `bench_frame_incremental_diff` -- render 100 frames with incremental diff
   - `bench_frame_bayesian_strategy` -- render 100 frames with Bayesian strategy
2. Conformal coverage validation (if Track G is complete):
   - For the Bayesian strategy's frame times, verify that the conformal prediction interval contains >= 90% of actual frame times (distribution-free coverage guarantee).
3. Create a frame-time golden test: render a fixed sequence of 50 frames with known data mutations, record the strategy decisions. Verify they match the golden fixture.

**Risks / Safe Mode**

- Risk: Frame benchmarks are noisy on CI. Mitigation: Use criterion's statistical significance test; only flag regressions > 20%.
- Fallback trigger: None (pure measurement).

**Tests (4 required)**

1. `frame_bench_bayesian_not_slower_than_full` -- Bayesian p99 <= 1.2 * Full p99
2. `frame_bench_incremental_faster_on_stable` -- Incremental p50 < Full p50 for stable frames
3. `conformal_coverage_90pct` -- >= 90% of frame times within predicted interval
4. `golden_strategy_decisions_match` -- fixed sequence produces same decisions as fixture","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- Criterion suite benchmarks at least three representative rendering scenarios with/without strategy
- Bench outputs include p50/p95/p99 frame latency and tail-jitter metrics with reproducible seeds
- Deterministic golden tests verify strategy decisions on fixed frame-state traces
- Conformal coverage validation is executed when Track G is enabled and reports empirical coverage/confidence
- Unit tests verify benchmark harness and coverage-calculation helper correctness
- E2E scenario validates no UX regression under benchmarked load pattern
- Diagnostics include per-scenario config, confidence intervals, and artifact locations","notes":"## Plan-Space Review Findings (2026-02-13)

**FIX: Conformal coverage test is conditional.** Test 3 (`conformal_coverage_90pct`) depends on G.2 completion. Mark as `#[ignore]` with comment "requires Track G" until G.2 lands. Do not make it a hard requirement.

**FIX: Golden test fragility.** The golden test (test 4) for a probabilistic system is inherently fragile — any model tuning changes all golden values. Mitigate: the golden fixture should capture the ACTIONS chosen (not the raw posterior values), and compare action sequences. Actions are discrete and more stable than continuous posteriors.

**FIX: Add `[[bench]]` entry.** The server crate's `Cargo.toml` needs a `[[bench]] name = "frame_bench" harness = false` entry. Note this in scope.

**FIX: CI-safe timing assertions.** Replace hard ratio thresholds (e.g., ">= 1.5x") with `#[ignore]` gated performance tests that require explicit `--ignored` flag. CI runs correctness tests only; perf tests run manually or in dedicated perf CI.

**Additional tests:**
5. `bench_observe_overhead` — benchmark `strategy.observe()` in isolation, verify < 1us p99
6. `bench_cold_start` — first 10 frames with uniform prior, verify no worse than full-diff
7. `bench_varying_data_sizes` — benchmark with 10, 100, 1000 agents to verify scaling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:45.196698361Z","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:49:53.189559467Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:49:53.189490989Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2zq8o","depends_on_id":"br-194ry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:54:23.834413351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2zq8o","depends_on_id":"br-v3hid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:47:18.318453904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-302z","title":"force_release notification never delivered: uses create_message without recipients","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-09T18:01:53.850476070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:02:51.158628959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T18:02:51.158610073Z","close_reason":"Fixed: replaced create_message with create_message_with_recipients to actually deliver notification to holder's inbox","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
